qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via IPv6-mapped IPv4 addresses bypasses IP blacklist in CORS proxy
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an IP-address blacklist bypass via IPv6-mapped IPv4 addresses in the CORS proxy's SSRF protection. The advisory describes a domain validation bypass in the proxyableDomains allowlist where 'maliciousexample.com' bypasses validation when 'example.com' is allowed. These are different vulnerabilities: the finding exploits the range_check library's handling of IPv6-mapped addresses, while the advisory exploits a string suffix check bug in domain allowlist validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-w789-49fc-v8hr) describes a domain validation bypass in TerriaJS-Server's proxy allowlist where the validation checks only if a hostname ends with an allowed domain, allowing 'maliciousexample.com' to bypass when 'example.com' is allowed. The ssrf-ipv6-mapped-ipv4-bypass finding describes a different SSRF bypass via IPv6-mapped IPv4 addresses that evade an IP-address blacklist. The finding does not identify the vulnerable domain suffix-check logic described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-27818 describes proxyableDomains validation bypass where suffix check allows maliciousexample.com when example.com is allowed
Advisory patch commit 3aaa5d9717162b245ae4569232bbe7d8673c913f fixes domain validation logic
ssrf-ipv6-mapped-ipv4-bypass finding targets IP blacklist bypass via IPv6-mapped addresses, not domain allowlist suffix validation
